titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Program on Non-Profit Organizations, Yale University, Records
Program on Non-Profit Organizations, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday May 14, 2020 at 07:36
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Program on Non-Profit Organizations, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records consist of subject and personnel files, including computer files, of the Program on Non-Profit Organizations (PONPO) at Yale. The records document all aspects of the program including administration, research, and finance.

corpname.part: Yale University. Institution for Social and Policy Studies. Program on Non-Profit Organizations

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 37 (YRG 37), Records of associations, institutes, centers, and organizations affiliated with Yale.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The records were transferred from the Yale School of Management, 2004.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the materials is restricted. See Collection Contents for details.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Program on Non-Profit Organizations, Yale University, Records (RU 950). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Program on Non-Profit Organizations (PONPO) at Yale University was founded in 1978 to foster interdisciplinary research aimed at developing an understanding of nonprofit organizations and their role in economic and political life. Originally an initiative of Yale president Kingman Brewster, PONPO was first directed by Professor John G. Simon of the Law School in cooperation with Charles E. Lindblom of the Political Science and Economics Departments and director of the Institute for Social and Policy Studies. PONPO spent most of its life at the Institute, but was hosted at the Divinity School from 1998 to 2002. PONPO is currently directed jointly by the Yale School of Management and the Yale Center for International and Area Studies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of subject and personnel files, including computer files, of the Program on Non-Profit Organizations (PONPO) at Yale. The records document all aspects of the program including administration, research, and finance.

corpname.part: Yale University. Institution for Social and Policy Studies. Program on Non-Profit Organizations
